niche: Developer career in the AI era
queryStrategy: Pivoted from AI-tools niche to Career (blog has only 6 Career posts vs 52 AI). Focus: how the dev career ladder is actually changing in 2026 — hiring data (LinkedIn -15% YoY, -28% entry-level since 2022), salary deltas (ML specialists 44% premium vs 27% in 2022), cognitive load (HBR 'AI brain fry'), interview format shifts (Google's AI-assisted pilot), and new playable paths (one-person SaaS, IC track resurgence). Angles others haven't covered with data + opinion.

topics.title: The Hollow Career Ladder — Why Entry-Level Dev Jobs Dropped 28% and How Juniors Break In Anyway
topics.whyTrending: LinkedIn data: new SWE postings -15% YoY in Jan-Feb 2026, entry-level -28% from 2022 peaks. Senior+AI replacing junior tier. Massive search intent from grads, bootcampers, career-switchers; nobody has a tactical playbook yet for breaking the cycle.

topics.title: The Specialization Premium — Why ML Engineers Now Earn 44% More Than Generalists (and What to Specialize In)
topics.whyTrending: ML specialist premium widened from 27% in 2022 to 44% in 2026. AI engineers earning $145K-$310K base, $400K+ TC at FAANG. Remote roles settled at 80-95% of Bay Area rates — geo no longer the moat, specialization is. High commercial-intent searches around salary + specialization paths.

topics.title: AI Brain Fry — The 2026 Burnout Pattern That Hits Developers Who Use AI Tools the Hardest
topics.whyTrending: March 2026 HBR study of ~1,500 workers identified 'AI brain fry': higher cognitive load from supervising AI outputs, comparing drafts, verifying facts, managing tool stacks. 77% of employees report record-high burnout. Quitting intent correlates with AI usage. Fresh phenomenon, almost no practical guides on prevention.

topics.title: Interview Roulette — Which Companies Let You Use AI in the Coding Round in 2026 (and How to Win Either Format)
topics.whyTrending: Google piloting AI-assisted coding interviews for junior/mid roles on select US teams. ~25% of NY employers now allow AI in technical interviews (could hit 50%). Canva, Rippling, Red Hat, Meta, Shopify already allow. Others explicitly forbid. Take-home tests dying as a signal. Candidates desperate for guidance — perfect SEO + tactical content.

topics.title: From Senior Dev to One-Person SaaS — A 2026 Playbook for the Solo Founder Path
topics.whyTrending: Pieter Levels $3M ARR solo, Danny Postma's HeadshotPro $3.6M ARR solo, Lisbon founder hit $10K MRR in 47 days with zero hand-written code. Tech stack $3-12K/yr, 70%+ margins. Most devs see the headlines but lack the concrete transition framework from FTE to solo.

topics.title: The IC Track Is Back — Why Staff Engineers Now Out-Earn Engineering Managers by 15-25% in 2026
topics.whyTrending: Staff Engineers $250K-$450K TC, Principal $400K-$600K+ at FAANG, regularly out-earning EMs. AI-first companies finally treating IC ladder as valid alternative path. 'Senior → Staff' jump is the hardest promotion in tech — concrete advice scarce. Strong evergreen + timely angle.

topics.title: Eval Skills Beat Coding Skills — The Trust-Gap Hire Companies Want in 2026 (And How to Become One)
topics.whyTrending: 84% of devs adopted AI tools, only 29% trust the output. Companies hiring for 'verify, constrain, evaluate AI' — failure-mode intuition becoming the new differentiator. Almost no career content frames eval discipline as a hireable skill yet. Strong opinion piece potential.

topics.title: Building in Public as a 2026 Career Strategy — Why the GitHub Profile Lost to the Twitter Timeline
topics.whyTrending: Build-in-public momentum continues; hiring managers vet by Twitter/LinkedIn presence over LeetCode. Devs who ship demos publicly attract inbound roles + clients. Counter-narrative angle: how to do it without burning out or oversharing. Opinionated, sparks debate.

topics.title: The Two-Year Pivot — A Senior Dev's Roadmap from React to AI Engineer (Without a PhD)
topics.whyTrending: Entry-level GenAI postings +200% YoY, but most devs don't know how to bridge from app dev to AI eng. RAG, vector DBs (Pinecone/Milvus/Weaviate), eval discipline are the gateway skills. 6-12 month realistic timeline. High commercial intent, low-quality competition.

topics.title: Layoff Survival Math — What 2026's 52K Q1 Tech Cuts Actually Mean for Your Career Plan
topics.whyTrending: Q1 2026: 52,050 tech cuts (highest since 2023). Only 20% explicitly AI-attributed — rest is over-hire correction + AI capex funding. BLS still projects +15% SWE growth 2024-34. Devs are reading layoff headlines without context. Data-driven, calm-the-panic angle stands out.
